<!-- generated by claude-dev-kit: edit .claude/kit.overlay/skills/critique-delta/SKILL.md, not this file -->

# critique-delta — re-critique on the diff, not the whole artifact

The `plan-consensus` loop (and any cold-critic-to-consensus loop) re-spawns a **new** critic each
round, and each critic reads the **full artifact by absolute path**. After round 1 that re-reads text
the critic already cleared. Consensus loops converge — round N touches a few sections, not the whole
artifact — so bill the critic for the **change size, not the artifact size**.

This skill is the round-≥2 step. Round 1 stays a full cold critique (you need the baseline). It only
works because the `critic` agent has a `[DELTA]` mode: given the diff + changed ranges, it Reads only
those ranges and refuses to false-pass on missing context (`needs-full-doc` finding). Read the critic's
`## [DELTA] re-critique mode` section — this skill is the *caller* side of that contract.

It is artifact-agnostic: a **doc** is a git file with history; a **plan** round N−1→N is the same delta
case (prior plan = base, revised plan = delta). See `docs/cold-critic-token-economy.md` for the lever
catalogue and the trial evidence behind it.

## State lives in files, never in context

Per artifact under review, keep:
- `scratchpad/<artifact>/findings-rN.md` — critic report of round N (the F-ids + severities + status).
- A baseline source per round: a git tag/commit (a tracked doc), or an explicit prior-round file (a plan
  that has no commit of its own), so the diff is exact.

## The delta round (shared by both modes)

For a **plan** under the consensus loop, `bin/kit-critic --delta <prior-round-plan> <revised-plan>
[prior-findings]` automates steps 1–3 below (diff → ranges → `[DELTA]` payload) AND preserves the
program-attested consensus marker + model fallback — prefer it over a hand-assembled Agent-tool spawn.
The manual steps remain the spec for a **doc** (git-tracked baseline) or any caller without the launcher:

1. **Build the diff** against the previous round's baseline:
   `git diff <prev-baseline> -- <artifact-path>` (or `diff <prev-plan-file> <revised-plan-file>`).
2. **Derive the changed line ranges** from the hunk headers (`@@ -a,b +c,d @@` → ranges `c..c+d`).
3. **Assemble the `[DELTA]` payload** = artifact **absolute path** + the changed ranges + the diff
   hunks, plus the prior-findings file as `[PRIOR FINDINGS]`.
4. **Critic task:** rule each prior F-id (`fixed`/`withdrawn`/`open`) against the diff, AND audit the
   changed section on BOTH axes — local correctness of the changed text itself, and what the change
   contradicts elsewhere — Reading the **enclosing section** of each range (not bare hunk lines), with
   the honesty guard ((R) reference-chase, (S) structural-escalate) live.
5. **Save** the report to `scratchpad/<artifact>/findings-r$N.md`, re-anchor the baseline, fix, repeat.

## Recall: run k, then union — never gate on one delta run

A single cold delta run surfaces only a **stochastic subset** of the findings (so does a single cold
full run — this is a property of cold critics, not a delta defect). For a recall-sensitive gate, run
**k ≥ 2–3** cold deltas and **union** their findings before you trust "nothing was dropped". This is the
"wider, not taller" union the consensus loop already favours, applied to the delta arm. Per-run economy
is real (`-50..75%` input on real-size artifacts); k-run economy is that saving ÷ k vs a k-run full —
still net-cheaper because each delta reads ~⅓ the input.

## The honesty guard — why delta doesn't silently false-pass

The cheapness of delta is bounded by one risk: a change interacting with **unchanged** text the critic
never read. The critic agent's `[DELTA]` mode carries the hardened guard; this skill assumes it:
- **(R) reference-chase** — a hunk that names a target outside the loaded ranges (a section ref, a
  field/symbol, a contract clause, a sibling artifact) obliges the critic to Read that target before
  ruling, or escalate `needs-full-doc`. No pass-by-absence.
- **(S) structural-edit escalation** — edits to a signature/contract field, a normative clause, or an
  invariant are NOT judged on the delta alone: read the full affected section + its sibling anchor, or
  escalate.
- **Ceiling (honest):** delta need NOT re-discover a pre-existing issue in an **unrelated** unchanged
  section, or a cross-target the hunk does not reference — that is `[PRIOR FINDINGS]` (round-1's job) and
  the periodic full sweep, not the delta round's. So a fair run **carries round-1's findings into round
  2**; comparing delta-fresh vs full-fresh with NO prior unfairly penalises the delta.

Termination is unchanged from the consensus protocol: every prior `bug`/`robustness` id explicitly
`fixed`/`withdrawn`, no new blocking finding, all else Fixed or Accepted.

## Mode A — same session

Contract + siblings are already in the main context → never re-Read them. The delta round runs as a
sub-task in the same context, not a fresh spawn.
- **Win:** zero re-read of static grounding.
- **Cost:** context is no longer "cold" — a same-session critic shares the author's blind spots and
  catches less. So keep **round 1 as a real cold fork** (Mode B); use same-session only for the
  converging delta rounds.

## Mode B — cold fork (default for round 1; use for big/dirty artifacts)

Each spawn starts empty, so it must read *something*. Do **not** hand it the full artifact/contract. Hand
exactly: a contract excerpt (not the full contract), `findings-r$((N-1)).md` as `[PRIOR FINDINGS]`, and
the `[DELTA]` payload (path + ranges + hunks). Coldness is preserved (clean adversarial stance) AND
billing scales with the delta. **Pass paths, never paste artifact bodies** — pasting risks the
placeholder/empty-artifact failure; the critic Reads the named ranges itself.

## When to use which
- **Round 1:** always Mode B (honest cold critic on the full artifact).
- **Rounds 2+:** Mode A if cheap and the artifact is small / converging; Mode B if you need max rigor or
  the artifact is large and churning. For a recall-gate, k cold Mode-B runs unioned (see above).

## Project glue (animata-pm)

For a **plan** under the consensus loop, the kit launcher runs the whole delta round for you — prefer it
over a hand-assembled Agent-tool spawn, because it keeps the program-attested `.plan-consensus` marker and
the opus fallback that the raw `subagent_type: critic` path drops:

    .claude-dev-kit/bin/kit-critic --delta <prior-round-snapshot> <current-plan> <prior-findings>

- **Artifacts.** Plans live at `.claude/plans/<name>.md`. The explicit delta baseline (a plan has no
  mid-loop commit) is a per-round snapshot at `.claude/plans/.consensus/<name>.r<N>.md` (gitignored) —
  `cp` the plan there right after each round's critique so the baseline is exactly the text that was
  critiqued. That `cp` is the project's "re-anchor the baseline" step (step 5 above); there is no git tag.
- **Findings.** The critic report lands at `.claude/.critic-report.md`; the machine-derived open-findings
  carry-file is `.claude/.critic-prior.md`
  (`python3 .claude-dev-kit/lib/critic-prior.py .claude/.critic-report.md > .claude/.critic-prior.md`).
- **Diff/ranges extraction is the launcher's job now** — no `rtk`/git-tag glue needed for plans.
  `kit-critic --delta` does steps 1–3 (diff → ranges → `[DELTA]` payload) internally. A git-tracked **doc**
  (not a plan) would instead use the manual `git diff <tag>` steps above.
- **Mode.** `kit-critic --delta` is a cold fork (Mode B): use it for round 1's revisions and for
  large/churning plans. For a recall-sensitive plan, invoke it k≥2 times and union the findings — the
  launcher is one run per invocation; coldness is preserved each time.
